methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $1.5B - $4.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Ultima Genomics
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $4B

private.name: Insitro
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $2.4B

private.name: Colossal Biosciences
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.5B

data.narrative:
DNA Script, a Series C–stage biotech company in the Biotech & Health sector, has raised $200M+ to date. The valuation analysis employs a peer set of 3 same-stage private comparables (Ultima Genomics, Insitro, Colossal Biosciences) and 3 public sector benchmarks (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-Progression analysis applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier to the $200M raised proxy, yielding $340M. Peer-Median anchoring across the 3 same-sector, same-stage comparables produces a $2.4B implied valuation, with individual peers ranging $1.5B to $4.0B. Public-Comp Anchoring cannot be applied due to absence of DNA Script revenue disclosure; the sector median P/S of 7.4x cannot be deployed without this input.

The two applicable methods triangulate to a median valuation of $1.4B, with a low-to-high range of $289M to $2.8B. The wide range reflects material uncertainty around growth trajectory and stage-specific risk. The Stage-Progression method, anchored only to capital raised, sits well below peer medians and likely understates fair value for a funded frontier-tech biotech. Peer medians appear more representative but carry execution and market risk typical of early-stage genomics. Revenue visibility and path to commercialization remain critical unknowns narrowing this band.
